# Juan Bautista Alonso Lareo

## Who I am
Juan Bautista Alonso Lareo is an entrepreneur and founder based in Buenos Aires, Argentina. Born in Montevideo, Uruguay, in 2001, he moved to Argentina at age three. He is the co-founder and CEO of Zoppa, a fashion marketplace, and a former Financial Planning intern at Naranja X (a leading Argentine fintech backed by Grupo Galicia).

## Education
- Business Administration, Universidad de San Andrés (UdeSA) — one of the top-ranked business schools in Latin America (2020–2024)
- UCLA Summer Program, Los Angeles (2018) — business of entertainment, media, and sports
- HarvardX — Entrepreneurship in Emerging Economies
- MITx — User Innovation and Entrepreneurship

## Professional experience
- **Founder & CEO, Zoppa** (2025) — fashion marketplace with a lean, low-cost operational model designed to compete with established players without replicating their fixed-cost infrastructure.
- **Financial Planning Intern, Naranja X** (2024) — selected from 7,500+ applicants (top 10). Maintained critical financial databases, built Sankey diagrams for board presentations, and worked with Excel, Power BI, and ERP systems.
- **Investment Fund** (2020–2023) — co-created a democratic investment fund with 8 partners. Decisions made by weighted vote on Slack. Achieved ~31% return over 26 months.
- **Cross-Border Commerce** (2023) — identified pricing inefficiencies between Argentina and Uruguay in sports supplements; built and sold through personal network.

## Interests & passions
- New technologies, especially artificial intelligence and blockchain
- Learning about successful companies and founders
- Sports: football, tennis, padel, golf, surfing, gym
- Podcasts and interviews with high-achieving professionals
